title: Linked Lists, Nodes, and Pointer Invariants · Adversarial-Input Lab
summary: Build a mutable work queue where stable node links matter more than random access. The scenario, data, constraints, and failure modes differ from the workshop. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of a linked structure with diagrammed invariants with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Trace singly and doubly linked node references without losing head, tail, or reachability.
Insert and delete at boundaries while preserving connectivity and size invariants.
Implement traversal with termination evidence and malformed-cycle protection.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
